short_description: Pixie's same-inside set applied on the legover base -- the bag passes behind the planted leg as the body executes the leg-over dex sequence.
execution_summary: Open with a toe set; the first kick lands same-side inside (pixie), sending the bag behind the supporting leg. The body completes the canonical legover motion -- the second leg passing over the supporting leg -- with the bag landing on a same-toe delay. The pixie opener is the only addition over canonical legover; the leg-over geometry stays intact.
learning_notes: Pixie's tight first kick competes with legover's natural leg-passing reach. The miss is a too-compact opener that crowds the leg-pass; keep the pixie set compact only on the foot, leaving the body free for the leg-over rotation.
prerequisite_notes: Legover (2 ADD) provides the leg-passing geometry. Smear (3 ADD, pixie + mirage; SCALE-5 pilot) and pigbeater (4 ADD, pixie + eggbeater; SCALE-1 pilot) cover pixie on rotational and deeper leg-over bases. PassBack records this row as 'Pixie near Legover' -- the `near` positional carries no ADD weight.

short_description: The symposium body line carries through mirage's rotational dex -- shoulders aligned and hips set on the symposium frame as the bag completes the in-to-out rotation.
execution_summary: Set the symposium posture before the rotation begins. The first kick drives outside the supporting leg in mirage's canonical pattern; the body holds the symposium line across the rotation; recovery lands on the opposite-toe delay with the posture intact. Symposium's signature is the held body alignment through the entire trick, not a momentary pose.
learning_notes: Posture collapse mid-rotation is the failure mode. Clean mirage finishes with the body somewhat relaxed; symposium-mirage demands the line stay locked across both kicks. Lock the posture before the rotation initiates, not after.
prerequisite_notes: Mirage (2 ADD) gives the rotational dex base. Symposium-mirage is the simplest symposium compound -- symposium-whirl (4 ADD, SCALE-2 pilot), paradox-symposium-whirl + spinning-symposium-whirl (5 ADD pilots), mullet (6 ADD pilot), and montage (7 ADD pilot) all build on this row's held-posture discipline.

short_description: Atomic on the mirage rotational base -- the atomic body angle delivers a +2 contribution on a rotational base, distinguishing mirage-base atomic compounds from non-rotational ones.
execution_summary: The body sets the atomic angle from the toe-set position; the first kick reaches outside the supporting leg in mirage's canonical pattern. The body's atomic-set body weighting commits across the rotation -- the second kick comes inside the supporting leg, completing the outside-then-inside dex pattern that defines the atom-smasher sequence. Recovery lands on the opposite-toe delay.
learning_notes: The atomic body angle is the load-bearing element -- it's what reaches the +2 ADD on a rotational base rather than the +1 atomic delivers on a non-rotational one. Half-committing the atomic body collapses the trick to a +1 mirage compound.
prerequisite_notes: Mirage (2 ADD) is the rotational foundation; the atomic body angle distinguishes atom-smasher from any +1 modifier on mirage. Atomic-butterfly (4 ADD, this batch) is the non-rotational sibling -- atomic delivers +1 there because butterfly isn't rotational. The two atomic rows side-by-side surface the rotational-bonus rule directly.

short_description: Where atom-smasher's mirage base gives atomic the +2 rotational bonus, atomic-butterfly gets the standard +1 -- butterfly is non-rotational, so atomic delivers its normal weight on the wing.
execution_summary: Open in butterfly's wing setup; the first kick reaches same-side outside in the canonical butterfly motion. The atomic set commits the body angle across the wing; the second kick reaches opposite-side outside as the body completes the wing pattern, landing in an opposite clipper cross-body delay. The atomic body weighting holds through both kicks -- it's a frame, not just the opener.
learning_notes: Atom-smasher (atomic + mirage) gets +2 from atomic because mirage is rotational; atomic-butterfly gets +1 because butterfly is not. The common miss isn't a body error -- it's expecting the rotational bonus to apply and over-committing the atomic body angle in compensation. Trust the +1 reading.
prerequisite_notes: Butterfly (3 ADD) is the wing foundation. Atom-smasher (4 ADD, this batch) gives the same atomic modifier on a rotational base for direct comparison. Scrambled-eggbeater (3 ADD, atomic + pickup; SCALE-1 pilot) is another non-rotational atomic compound. FM records this row under the alternate name 'Legbeater'.

short_description: Stepping's foot-relocation rides through drifter's rotation-stall -- the supporting foot taps to a new position mid-trick while the body completes the miraging clipper-stall.
execution_summary: Drifter's rotation initiates from clipper-stall; mid-rotation, the supporting foot taps or steps to a new position. The body continues through the rotation as the planted leg has moved; the clipper landing accepts the bag with the body now over the relocated support. Recovery holds the stall as the rotation completes.
learning_notes: Drifter's rotation needs the planted anchor through its first half; stepping too early steals that anchor. The step must arrive after the rotation is well-established. The stepping action is a beat inside the rotation, not a separate movement between phases.
prerequisite_notes: Drifter (3 ADD; SCALE-5 pilot) carries the rotation-stall integration. Stepping-whirl (4 ADD; SCALE-2 pilot) gives the stepping mechanic on a rotational base; ripwalk (4 ADD, stepping + butterfly; SCALE-5 pilot) covers stepping on a non-rotational base. Tombstone extends the stepping bridge to drifter.

short_description: Completing the paradox bridge to the torque family -- paradox direction commits across torque's rotational frame, the body resetting same-side at landing against the natural rotational drift.
execution_summary: Torque's rotation initiates through the kick sequence -- the body executes the canonical mirage-on-osis frame. The paradox marker forces the body to commit same-side across the rotation, against the natural opposite-side drift torque carries. The kick lands as the rotation completes; recovery holds the paradox commitment through the delay.
learning_notes: Torque's natural rotation drifts the body opposite-side, harder to override than on simpler bases. A half-committed paradox gets absorbed by torque's frame and the trick reads as a clean torque. The paradox direction must commit before the rotation builds momentum, not during.
prerequisite_notes: Torque (4 ADD) is the rotational frame. The paradox bridge now spans paradox-mirage (3 ADD, SCALE-5), paradox-whirl (4 ADD, SCALE-2), paradox-blender (5 ADD, SCALE-3), paradox-drifter (4 ADD, SCALE-5), and paradox-torque (this row, 5 ADD) -- paradox direction practiced on every major rotational base. Spinal-tap (5 ADD, torque + tapping; SCALE-1 pilot) is the same-tier torque sibling.
